item.title: "Today they killed my mother."
item.description: Today, the Russian military killed my mother, the person whom I loved more than my life ... in the morning they started shelling our village, blew up the military base, the shock wave threw me away from the window, the house shook, the electricity was gone .. We went to the city to withdraw money and buy food, returning home at the exit from Kharkiv, Russian tanks blocked the road, they fired at our car, we turned into a dead end and jumped out of the car, two guys jumped out to meet us, Russian soldiers, young, 18-20 years old .. who could barely held their weapons. .... We stayed there for 15 minutes, then they started shooting at us .. My mother was wounded in the arm, I heard her scream in pain .. And at that time a soldier threw me to the ground and shouted many times do not shoot ... They are ours ... He yelled at the Russian soldiers, don't shoot.. Heavy shooting began.. A dead soldier fell on me.. The second one was also shot... But my mother was silent... I got up and understood that she was dead.. She was hit in the head. .. I was left alone. They were shooting all around me, I grabbed my papers, etc. I hiding behind an iron booth ... Dad at that time was driving from Kyiv at all speeds, not yet knowing that my mother had been killed, and I was hiding so that they would not kill me . I sat for an hour and a half behind this booth, I was afraid to move. After a while, I I heard that the shooing had stopped and I crawled to the car and took a wounded soldier with me .. I turned the car along the curb and drove at full speed from the place of shooting. So I was left without a mother, without Irina Perlifon, whom so many loved and respected. And who was a great role model for me in this life, a person who never did anything bad to anyone, my very kind and purposeful beloved mother. I am writing this now and just crying out loud. I don’t believe it. I don’t know I survived, how I escaped and saved a person's life. But I couldn’t save my mother. My phone was hot from phone calls. I’ll tell you honestly, when I was lying on the ground among bullets and dead people, I understood that I would die. This is not a fairy tale, and not the news that you are shown, in our cities of Ukraine it is happening, it's a real hell... This is not even 2014... this is HELL... Source: https://www.instagram.com/karolisha_8/
item.dc:creator: aaaaaaaa0

item.title: Welp.
item.description: Thank you all for the kind words and encouragement. My dad packed up my cousins and they’re on the road to the Western border now. It’s bumper to bumper all the way to Moldova, and what would normally be a 6-8 hour drive will likely take 5x that. (But it’s my mom’s birthday, and my dad promised he would do everything to get to her today. He never breaks his promises. If anyone can make it, it’ll be my dad.)

item.dc:creator: FromTheBeyond

item.title: So yeah guys ... i'm from Ukraine
item.description:
Fuck Putin ... fuck war
I know you see a lot of this kind of videos today, but yeah ... i fucking live in here. I live in western part of Ukraine but even here people are, and i can't stress this enough, VERY scared and disturbed. We hear that Russians stopped their atacks to regroup right now, but we know for sure that they will continue. 1\2 of eastern Ukraine was under constant atacks and as a cherry to the top - we have enemy crawling from South (Crimea, Mariupol) and North-west (fucking Lukashenko). We are strong. We are proud to meet them with our chests. But holy fucking shit guys ... we are scared. P.S. I'm in love with Imgur comunity and very glad to see your support. I play and spread the love for DnD in my city, even if the game is not very popular. I was watching your elections and was very happy for your relief from Trumps stupendous rule. AND I SURE HOPE TO CONTINUE MY ADVENTURE IN YOUR WORLD. But even if i won't... i sure was glad to know you guys. Fuck war. Edit: Much thanks for this awesome support, my dudes. I'm not very happy for such post going most viral for sure, but omg, thanks. I just wanna say, that this is the start of something bigger, and I sure hope, that it will be a happy something. Again, ukrainians are strong, and we will fight back with all our might. Slava Ukraini!
disturbed cat tax

item.title: Welp
item.description: No pun intended but I hope this dies in usersub. I don't want much. I'm just trying to take the first step to be able to say this out loud. Last November I was home alone on my day off. I took a rope that my husband bought for shibari, walked into the garage, tied it to the top of our work out rack (it is bolted into the wall so it is really sturdy), and almost went down the sewerslide. My saving grace was that I couldn't figure out how long the rope needed to be so I jumped up to do a dead hang (again, no pun intended) from the top of the rack to estimate how much I would need. As I was hanging by my arms the gym equipment, tools, scrap wood, law mower, and everything in the garage transformed into visions of my husband, our daughter, our son, my best friend who was getting engaged the upcoming weekend, my family and all the things I was about to say bye to. I let go of the bar and just hit the ground. I laid there for a few hours just crying and thinking of how I was about to leave my husband alone to face what I couldn't handle even as a two-person team. I thought about my children coming home from school and my curious daughter being the one to see me first. The chaos and pain I was about to unleash. The trauma I was about to make. And I was so angry that I couldn't think of all these reasons not to do it BEFORE. I got myself up. Put everything as I found it. Took a shower. And moved on. But the more I pretend nothing happened the more guilt I feel. No need to worry about me today. I did go to the doctor and I have antidepressants that seem to be working. I'm not perfect but I'm working on it and doing better. I'm just venting here because this knowledge is a weight on my heart but I can't bring myself to say the words out loud yet. Once I can face the situation I will get some more peace. I'm just not ready. Vent here, say it out loud alone, then admit it to someone. Thanks for listening. National Suicide Hotline: 800-273-8255
item.dc:creator: jauntyseapearl

item.title: 80-year-old showed up to join the army, carrying with him a small case with 2 tshirts, a pair of extra pants, a toothbrush and a few sandwiches for lunch. He said he was doing it for his grandkids.

item.title: The Fog of War... (Ukraine Conflict Day One)
item.description:
Good morning Imgur. This one will be brief. It will be brief because we are in the fog of war. In such times I hate to tell you this, but really no one has any idea what is going on. Russia has invaded Ukraine. There are reports of explosions and fighting. Twitter will be filled with ArmA3 videos saying it’s footage from Ukraine. Front Page Edit: thank you usersub for being Imgur’S MVP’s still. Glad I was able to find something original on the issue.
Russia will say stuff. https://apnews.com/article/russia-ukraine-entertainment-media-europe-moscow-a760a2b6dcf5ef820cee7138336a5b3b Ukraine will say stuff. https://www.rferl.org/a/putin-ukraine-military-operation/31719680.html Footage will appear that may be real. Or may be fake. We won’t know for a while, a few days at least, but actual war ain’t like the movies. No one knows _exactly_ what is happening. Some can speculate. I won’t. But I hope to at least present you with some useful insights being missed in the heady coverage elsewhere.
It’s like as I type this? We got news that the Chernobyl Nuclear Site was bombed. And I was like ‘what the hell’. Now it appears a singular ‘waste site’ in that vast area was destroyed. Hate to say it but there is a BIBLICALLY large difference between the destroying a waste dump and destroying the old reactor. (Sighs) Click bait is going to be a thing. https://www.fxstreet.com/news/nuclear-waste-storage-facility-destroyed-in-chernobyl-amid-russia-ukraine-fighting-nbc-202202241451
If you are going to read stuff about what’s going on, just keep saying ‘this could be total bullshit, this could be total bullshit...’ as you do. It may turn out what you are reading is true but maintaining that degree of skepticism will help. Make it the new Imgur motto... ‘this could be total bullshit...’ for at least 24 hours on anything you hear. So if this is your first time dealing with a major geopolitical conflict allow me offer the following as a handy dandy survival guide for your mental health.
No this isn’t World War 3. The actual tag is insulting. It isn’t World War 3 because NATO has never signed the mutual defence pact with Ukraine, that it has signed with its members. We have said all along in the event of a Russian invasion, Ukraine fights alone. Please note, if this was Latvia? Every one of the NATO nation’s are legally obligated to defend them so yeah. It’s a fairly black and white issue really. So it won’t be WW3; just a vicious war in Eastern Europe. (He says- admitting he is gonna repeat this mantra with a scary look in his eye)
Russia will win simply because it has more men. That’s the ONLY reason it will win. Their artillery is decent but every other weapon system they own is 80’s technology held together by patriotism and glue. Russia is claiming to be hitting targets with high precision bombs. https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/02/24/russia-not-bombing-ukrainian-cities-military-claims-a76552 We know from Syria that the Russian definition of ‘high precision’ means if a missile actually lands within a mile of the target? They doing good.
But some pro-Russian writers are cackling that this is the war they have learned from the tactics used by NATO in the past. This article from the Jerusalem Post suggests that (there exists a pernicious body of Israelis who are basically Russians and suck deep of the ’Putin is great’ mantra; they have been licking their wounds ever since Daddy Vlad did a deal with Syria (who are violently anti-Israeli) but they coming out of the woodwork to all praise Russian ingenuity). https://www.jpost.com/international/article-698493 Please note: it is only two weeks ago that Russia actually condemned Israel for running bombing raids into Syria against Iranian targets. They demanded Israel respects Syrian borders. Seriously they said this. Also worth noting that Russia did its whole ‘threat’ towards Israel if they continued to do this. Israel’s response is the same as American response to Russian threats... utterly ignore him. https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-696138
You will see stories claiming the Russians are doing things in the Mediterranean or the Atlantic. Everytime you see those stories, feel free to completely ignore them. The Russian fleet is old and out of date. I saw one story only yesterday blustering about how Russia had FOUR ships in the Med. Four? https://news.usni.org/2022/02/22/russian-navy-cruisers-positioned-to-counter-u-s-french-and-italian-carrier-groups-in-the-mediterranean Slightly less than the number of AEGIS cruisers around the US aircraft carrier in the Med ALONE, and the Russian ships can’t match AEGIS. Nor any of the other NATO fleets from France, Italy, Turkey, Britain who outnumber and outgun them. And remember kids- Russia has only one working aircraft carrier, that was built over 40 years ago, has a terrifying tendency to yeet planes into the sea and is currently broken and being repaired. So anything saying ‘oooo Russia is active outside of Ukraine’ is either websites looking for click bait or Russian dipshits trying to make hide the fact their fleet is old, run down, falling apart and shite.
You will also see stories going ‘now China will now invade Taiwan’. Like this one. https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/taiwan-reports-nine-chinese-aircraft-its-air-defence-zone-2022-02-24/ Banana for scale? 9 Chinese planes did their usual ‘cutting corner routine’ through Taiwanese airspace. Cutting corners? Oh whenever you hear about Chinese fighter planes entering Taiwan airspace its them flying MILES from actual Taiwan. In yesterday’s case? They overflow the Pratas Islands. Where are the Pratas Islands?
So by all accounts it was a much smaller incursion than the last big one on January 23rd which saw 39 craft do that and Taiwan admit openly that “Taipei has not reported any unusual movements by Chinese forces”. We are going to see a lot of this. Articles like this one from The Atlantic magazine that basically says ‘Russia attacking Ukraine suggests dictators have all decided they going to just attack everyone’ and use that to get paid to actually write an article saying Beijing is going to invade Taiwan. An article that has to sulkily concede “That doesn’t mean a Chinese attack on Taiwan is imminent.” No shit Sherlock. https://www.theatlantic.com/international/archive/2022/02/vladimir-putin-ukraine-taiwan/622907/
I don’t mind us supporting Taiwan, but if they going to inflate threats cos they want attention on them and not Ukraine, they can go to hell. It’s hard enough dealing with Chinese and Russian state actors trying to ‘play us’ right now. If Taiwan decides they want to manipulate us as well? (Facepalm) China is NOT going to invade Taiwan anytime soon OK?
China’s involvement in the Ukrainian conflict is interesting. It is bring reported that China was utterly taken by surprise by Putin going into Ukraine. They were running the whole ‘Russia only wants peace America is stoking this’ line right up until Russia went in. They were so sure of this they did NOT advise the 6,000 or so Chinese nationals in Ukraine they should evacuate. Leading to those 6,000 now on Weibo going ‘help us’ and China saying ‘stay home, you’re safe at home’ and possibly hoping the Russians don’t blow up any.
China won’t join any sanctions unless they are approved by the UN BUT they are kinda stuck. China has spent its entire modern existence saying nations shouldn’t invade other nations. And is torn between a possible geopolitical alliance with Russia and going against this principle. It will be interesting to see how this goes. Russia has made China lose face. Just a little. And they do NOT like to lose face. https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/02/24/ukraine-china-russia-invasion-reaction/
You will see stories/voices saying sanctions don’t work. Don’t believe them. Sanctions DO have an impact. Proof? Russia is already under a bunch of sanctions because they invaded Crimea/blew up an innocent Air Malaysia jet plane by handing an anti-air missile system to redneck yahoos from Donbas/endlessly launched cyber attacks upon America. Did they cause mass starvation on the streets of Moscow. No. But they did force Putin to blow his entire strategic reserves of currency. They have helped utterly cripple the Russian space industry. They are the reason Russia can’t afford a new aircraft carrier.
It’s worth remembering that Russian economy is walking a tightrope right now. https://carnegiemoscow.org/2021/11/24/coming-deluge-russia-s-looming-lost-decade-of-unpaid-bills-and-economic-stagnation-pub-85852 There is the belief now that Putin is basically JUST keeping the economy ticking along until 2036 and then the whole nation will come crashing down. And four weeks ago folks were saying he knows sanctions are going to hurt him. He is sacrificing economics to grab what he can before he can’t afford to anymore. https://www.politico.eu/article/vladimir-putin-gamble-russia-economy-ukraine/ I may do a full guide to the sanctions when they are finally revealed over the next few days.
But sanctions can and will punish Putin. We need to refute anyone who says they will hurt us (they won’t) and anyone who says they don’t work (they do- it’s just that it’s slow to hit). Wanna really hit Putin? Watch out for a mention of the ‘primary Bond Market’.
Finally, when Kenya speaks on behalf of Africa and says this attack is pure ‘old Empire colonialism’ it is fair to say that everyone, except the people who support Russia, condemns Russia in this without reservation, without hesitation, without pause. https://www.npr.org/2022/02/22/1082334172/kenya-security-council-russia Look out for people supporting Russia in your own nations. This will be invaluable moving forward for the safety of your families and communities.
Beyond this there is literally nothing I can add. Again, I could cite reports I’ve picked up but it’s all the fog of war. Ignore Twitter. No, seriously, ignore Twitter. It’s only useful purpose is to allow folks post footage of moments they don’t understand anymore then we do. They are on the ground, and they are as in the dark as we are. That’s the reality of war. Always has been. When it comes to analysing what is happening in a war? The ONLY people who need real-time information are soldiers. The rest of us should possibly just wait. Or join in the wild speculation frenzy, allowing ourselves get giddy with the dark intoxication of a war, feeling like we are experts. You do you boo. I ain’t judging ya. Chances are between the time of me writing this and the time of you reading this the situation could have changed.
The real question is when Russia wins (and unless Ukraine manages to inflict biblically high levels of casualties Russia WILL win), then what happens? It could be that Ukraine fights hard and manages to inflict staggering losses upon the Russians as the RAND corp suggested last December https://www.rand.org/blog/2021/12/expect-shock-and-awe-if-russia-invades-ukraine.html But we don’t know. If Ukraine surrenders, then what? Does he expect Ukrainians to vote for pro-Russian candidates? Will he respond to opposition figures in Ukraine the same way he responded to his own opposition figures and just jail them? He will have to replace the Ukrainian police force with his army. Which means he intends to keep his troops in Ukraine. How will he cope if an insurgency arises? Will he carpet bomb regions where insurgency happens? Will we in the West give the insurgents weapons? How will Russia respond to that?
If our sanctions are hurting him and occupation is expensive and Ukrainian insurgents have access to NATO weapons... how then will the mean and vicious Russian bear respond? There be ‘interesting times’ ahead for all of us I am afraid. Don’t panic. Breath. This too will pass.
Yes, Ukraine shouldn’t have to face this. Agreed. But geopolitics is ruthless. Also prepare yourselves for a long haul. Even if Russia wins this within a few days (and I ain’t convinced they will win as easily as some assume) Russian ‘occupation’ means this story could be with us until 2023. I’m in no rush to analyse this for a while. I don’t like war porn. But the Ukrainian story isn’t gonna go anywhere. Go treat yourself Imgur if you can. We all deserve a treat today. -@TheFeck
There could be TWO pings this week as I was working on something completely different when the balloon went up in Ukraine. Expect a non Ukraine related post tomorrow. Sorry for breaking my one ping per week max rule. Full list of all my posts so far: https://imgur.com/gallery/aTASLQH All my posts are little more than me trying to make sense of the world. I ain’t smart, I ain’t anything special, I’m just a dude on Imgur looking out and going ‘what the fuck?’ a LOT. As such I will continue to do my posts regardless, but if you feel inclined, you can click on the link below and buy me a coffee. Your support actually makes a difference. https://www.buymeacoffee.com/Thefeck
item.dc:creator: thefeckamisigningupwithfacebook

item.title: We could all use a nap hole

item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7
